raw_text:
Senior DevOps Engineer
CloudVenture Technologies — Enabling teams to build, deploy, and scale with

confidence.

Location

Remote (US-based)

Employment Type

Full-time

Compensation

$165,000 - $215,000 per year

The Opportunity

We are seeking an experienced Senior DevOps Engineer to join our
infrastructure and platform team. In this role, you will lead the design
and implementation of scalable, reliable cloud infrastructure and CI/CD

systems that support our rapidly growing customer base. You'll work on
complex infrastructure challenges, mentor junior team members, and drive

best practices across our organization. This position requires 5-8 years of
professional DevOps/SRE experience and deep expertise in cloud platforms,

containerization, and infrastructure-as-code.

About CloudVenture Technologies

CloudVenture Technologies is a mid-stage SaaS infrastructure company
founded in 2018, helping enterprises modernize their deployment pipelines

and cloud operations. We serve over 500 organizations across North America
and Europe, providing cutting-edge DevOps tooling and consulting services

that streamline infrastructure automation, CI/CD practices, and cloud-
native deployments.

Day-to-Day

Design, implement, and maintain Kubernetes clusters running on AWS EKS
and GCP GKE, ensuring high availability, security, and cost optimization

across multi-cloud environments.

Lead the evolution of our CI/CD infrastructure using Jenkins and GitHub
Actions, optimizing pipeline performance and reducing deployment times

from minutes to seconds.

Develop and manage infrastructure-as-code using Terraform, managing state

files securely and establishing standards for reusable modules across the
organization.

Write and maintain critical automation scripts in Python and Bash for

infrastructure provisioning, monitoring alerts, and incident response
automation.

Mentor and guide junior DevOps engineers on best practices for Docker

containerization, Kubernetes deployments, and infrastructure patterns;

 conduct code reviews on infrastructure projects.

Partner with development teams to troubleshoot production issues,
optimize application performance, and implement monitoring solutions

using Prometheus and Grafana.

Establish and enforce security policies for infrastructure, managing IAM

roles, secrets management, and implementing infrastructure compliance
scanning.

Drive architectural improvements and technical initiatives that reduce

operational overhead and improve system reliability across our platform.

Participate in on-call rotation, responding to and resolving critical
infrastructure incidents within agreed SLAs.

You Have

5-8 years of hands-on professional experience in DevOps, Site Reliability
Engineering (SRE), or infrastructure engineering roles.

Advanced proficiency with Kubernetes including cluster administration,
workload management, and troubleshooting production clusters.

Strong expertise with at least one major cloud provider (AWS or GCP),
including VPC management, IAM, and cloud-native services.

Demonstrated expertise writing infrastructure-as-code using Terraform,
managing complex multi-environment deployments.

Proficiency in Python and Bash scripting for automation, configuration

management, and operational tooling.

Deep understanding of Docker containerization, image optimization, and
container orchestration patterns.

Experience implementing and maintaining CI/CD pipelines using tools like
Jenkins, GitHub Actions, or GitLab CI.

Strong problem-solving skills and proven ability to design and implement
solutions for complex infrastructure challenges.

Bonus

Experience with multi-cloud strategies and active use of both AWS and GCP
in production environments.

Familiarity with infrastructure monitoring and observability stacks
including Prometheus, Grafana, ELK, or Datadog.

Previous experience mentoring and leading junior engineers or
contributing to engineering culture development.

Knowledge of infrastructure security practices, secrets management
(HashiCorp Vault), and compliance frameworks (SOC 2, HIPAA).

Contributions to open-source DevOps projects or active participation in

the DevOps community.

 Experience with service mesh technologies (Istio) or advanced networking
in Kubernetes environments.

Our Stack

Kubernetes   Docker   Terraform   AWS (EKS, EC2, VPC, IAM, S3)   GCP (GKE, Compute Engine, Cloud Storage)

Jenkins   GitHub Actions   Python   Bash   Prometheus & Grafana

What We Offer

Competitive salary and equity package with annual stock option grants.

Comprehensive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with 90%
employer coverage.

Unlimited PTO and flexible work-from-home policy with optional co-working
stipend.

Professional development budget ($3,000/year) for training,

certifications, and conferences.

Generous parental leave (16 weeks) and family planning support.

Free access to cloud sandbox environments for personal learning and
experimentation.

Quarterly team offsites and annual company retreat to a desirable

location.

Mental health support including counseling services and wellness stipend.

Apply

To apply, submit your resume, a cover letter highlighting your most
relevant DevOps experience, and a portfolio or GitHub profile showcasing

your infrastructure projects (optional but encouraged). Apply via our
careers page at careers.cloudventure-tech.com or email your application to

hiring@cloudventure-tech.com with the subject 'Senior DevOps Engineer
Application'. We review applications on a rolling basis and will contact

qualified candidates within 1 week.
